description_long: This hardware store occupies the ground floor of a multi-story brick and stucco building, with a prominent red and blue storefront sign reading 'HAZARD & CO.' and large display windows. The facade features two distinct sections: one with exposed red brick and another with a plain, light gray finish, each with vertically aligned sash windows. The roof is pitched and covered in dark shingles, with multiple chimneys and parapet walls visible from above. The ground level includes three separate storefronts, with the hardware store on the left, a central garage-style entrance, and another shop on the right. The windows above the hardware store are symmetrically arranged, each with white trim and sills. The overall structure is typical of older urban commercial buildings, blending utilitarian and traditional design elements.
description: A multi-story brick and stucco commercial building with a red and blue hardware store storefront, large display windows, and traditional sash windows above.
description_view_0: The view shows the brick facade with two upper floors of sash windows and the 'HAZARD & CO.' hardware store sign at street level.
description_view_1: This view reveals the full front of the building, including the hardware store, a central garage entrance, and an adjacent shop, as well as the contrasting brick and gray facades.
description_view_2: The rear view displays a plain, unadorned wall with no windows or doors, highlighting the building's depth and roof structure.
description_view_3: This side view shows the brick and gray facades meeting at a corner, with visible windows and a back door on the gray section.
attribute_storefront_signage: A red and blue sign with white text reading 'HAZARD & CO.' is mounted above the hardware store entrance, with a simple, bold font and no visible logos.
attribute_storefront_layout: The storefront features a large display window and a single entrance door beneath the signage, with the hardware store occupying the leftmost section of the building. Adjacent are a central garage-style entrance and another shopfront, all aligned at street level.
